title: Projections return stale data after materialized column modification
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
When a materialized column is modified using `ALTER TABLE MODIFY COLUMN` and projections are subsequently re-materialized, the projections continue to return stale data from before the column modification. Direct table queries (with projections disabled) return the correct updated data, but queries that use projections return outdated results.
This is a silent data correctness issue where users get incorrect query results without any error or warning. The projection appears to cache the old materialized column values and doesn't refresh them properly when `ALTER TABLE MATERIALIZE PROJECTION` is executed after a column modification.
A reproducer is available at: https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/95ccd7b8-a1e5-4e49-8133-32e1c878f3ce
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Using `25.6.2`, but it also happens in the most recent release - `25.8.2.29`
Steps to reproduce:
- Create table
- Insert data into it
- Add materialized column and materialize it
- Add projection
- Update materialized column and materialize it
- Materialize projection
- Queries reading from the projection don't reflect the last values of the materialized column
```sql
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS job_sample;
CREATE TABLE job_sample (
id UInt32,
posted_at Date,
discovered_at DateTime64(3, 'UTC'),
url String,
title String,
company_name String,
description String
) ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY (posted_at, discovered_at)
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(posted_at);
-- Insert sample data from S3 parquet file
INSERT INTO job_sample SELECT * FROM s3('https://media.theirstack.com/jobs/jan-2025-sample.parquet', 'Parquet');
-- Add materialized column with case-sensitive regex
ALTER TABLE job_sample ADD COLUMN description_matches_python UInt8 MATERIALIZED match(description, 'Python')
SETTINGS mutations_sync = 2, alter_sync = 2;
ALTER TABLE job_sample MATERIALIZE COLUMN description_matches_python
SETTINGS mutations_sync = 2, alter_sync = 2;
-- Check initial matches (case-sensitive "Python")
SELECT company_name, count(*) AS initial_matches
FROM job_sample
WHERE description_matches_python = 1
GROUP BY company_name
ORDER BY count(*) DESC
LIMIT 10;
-- Check count for specific company
SELECT count(*) AS jobs_via_dice_initial
FROM job_sample
WHERE company_name = 'Jobs via Dice' AND description_matches_python = 1;
-- returns 753 (correct data)
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE job_sample (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, posted_at DATE, discovered_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, url TEXT, title TEXT, company_name TEXT, description TEXT);
COPY job_sample (posted_at, discovered_at, url, title, company_name, description) FROM 'https://media.theirstack.com/jobs/jan-2025-sample.parquet' WITH (FORMAT 'parquet');
ALTER TABLE job_sample ADD COLUMN description_matches_python BOOLEAN;
SELECT company_name, COUNT(*) AS initial_matches FROM job_sample WHERE description_matches_python = TRUE GROUP BY company_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 10;
key_bug_features: Projections return stale data after column modification
